Run stop/transcribe work in background thread for instant UI update

The rumps callback blocks the main run loop, preventing the wait icon
from appearing until all work completes. Move blocking work (SIGINT,
stdout read, transcription, clipboard) to a background thread so the
icon updates immediately on click.

commit.message:
fix(statusline): rate limit handling for API calls

Previously, when the limits API returned 429, the background subprocess
silently caught the exception and wrote nothing. Since no cache was
written, every subsequent statusline invocation (~5s) retried the API
call, creating a sustained 429 storm.

Three-layer fix:
1. _BG_SCRIPT: on any failure, touch existing cache (extends stale
   data) or write {} — prevents retries for cache TTL
2. Limits payload: catch 429 specifically, read Retry-After header,
   write cooldown file with the server-specified delay (or 5min default)
3. _cached_json: check cooldown file before triggering refresh —
   respects server-requested backoff period

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Migrate choropleth maps from Plotly to react-simple-maps

Replace react-plotly.js choropleth + scattergeo traces with
react-simple-maps SVG rendering for better mobile support and
full d3-scale-chromatic colorscale access.

Key improvements:
- SVG viewBox scaling: entire map (fills, borders, labels) scales
  as a single unit — no re-projection artefacts on mobile
- d3-scale-chromatic interpolators used directly: contrast-adaptive
  text now parses the actual interpolated CSS colour rather than
  using hardcoded stop tables
- Proper hover tooltip (mouse) + tap-to-reveal (touch) via absolute
  div positioned relative to the map container
- CSS gradient colorbar replaces Plotly colorbar — simpler and
  dark-mode aware
- Annotations auto-hidden below 768 px viewport width
- No-data states still render with a neutral fill via Geography
  fill prop (no base trace workaround needed)
- Bundle: state-maps page ~47 kB vs Plotly's dynamic ~3 MB load

Packages added: react-simple-maps, d3-scale-chromatic,
@types/react-simple-maps, @types/d3-scale-chromatic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Subscribers UI: red/yellow shield icons, Skip WAN and Re-check buttons

Fix bot games stuck active: force resign on timeout, fix draw race

server-side game, leaving it stuck active. Now sends game:resign.

offer, with 5s fallback resign. Added overflow:hidden to chat column
to prevent chatbox growing beyond board height.

commit.message:
Fix launchctl audio quality and make restart reliability

Add ProcessType Interactive to plist template so launchd runs the
process at full priority. Without this, ffmpeg's avfoundation init
takes ~3-4s on Apple Silicon efficiency cores, losing most of the
recording window.

Make restart now regenerates the plist from template when it changes
and copies it to LaunchAgents before loading.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix auth flow: run setup-token inside container, not on host (#58)

The Tauri app was running `claude setup-token` directly on the host,
failing with "program not found" because Claude Code is inside the
container. Now delegates to the CLI's `auth` command.

- App: delegate to CLI `auth`, auto-open auth URL in system browser,
  scan both stdout/stderr, deduplicate browser opens with AtomicBool,
  generalize `collect_lines()` with `on_line` callback
- CLI Linux: remove `-it` from docker run (no TTY needed), pipe
  stdout/stderr through pass-through threads that print to terminal
  AND auto-open browser, deduplicate with AtomicBool
- CLI macOS: drop SSH `-t` for auth (no TTY needed, fails from app)
- Fix Windows `cmd /c start` URL truncation (empty title arg for `&`)

Co-authored-by: elyxlz <elyxlz@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: mark stories DONE on completion for pipeline resume (v2.0.32)

The pipeline's PLAN phase checks story files for `status: DONE` to skip
already-completed work, but the FORGE phase never wrote this status back.
Re-runs would re-execute all stories instead of only the pending ones.

Now `markStoryDone()` updates the story file from PENDING to DONE after
successful completion, enabling proper resume on re-run.

Also fixes getAnvilScript() Windows platform check in framework.ts and
adds log_level to test mock configs.

382 tests passing (2 new: story-mark-done, pipeline-resume).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add unity-articulation-physics and robotics-sim-debug skills

Two new skills for robot simulation development:

- unity-articulation-physics: Setting up ArticulationBody hierarchies
  with correct joint sign conventions, FBX-safe direction derivation,
  servo drive parameters, and anchor rotation formulas.

- robotics-sim-debug: Systematic debugging methodology using telemetry
  CSV analysis, diagnostic logging patterns, sign chain tracing, and
  mathematical verification of gait/IK control chains.

Both skills encode lessons learned from building a hexapod walking
simulation with 18 revolute joints on an FBX-imported model.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix cross-platform CLI inconsistencies and ANSI noise (#59)

* Suppress ANSI progress noise on Linux and macOS (#57 follow-up)

The previous fix only covered Windows. The same issue affects all
platforms when the Tauri app pipes CLI stdout/stderr:

- Linux: docker pull/build output ANSI progress bars through inherited
  stdio. Use docker_quiet for pull, suppress build output.
- macOS: curl and tar output progress through inherited stdio. Suppress
  both. VM image download/extract messages go to stderr.

Progress messages switched from println to eprintln so they go to
Tauri's log stream instead of corrupting captured stdout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix cross-platform inconsistencies in CLI

- macOS: add missing agent_ready and ws_port to StatusJson (matches Linux
  and Tauri app's expected schema)
- All platforms: progress messages use eprintln! so they go to stderr
  instead of stdout, preventing corruption of structured output (JSON)
  when Tauri pipes stdout
- Windows: "waiting for services" dots go to stderr

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: elyxlz <elyxlz@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace requests with Selenium for Booking.com scraping

requests+BS4 approach fails with HTTP 202 on every city because
Booking.com now renders search results client-side via JavaScript.
Selenium with headless Chrome resolves this by executing JS natively.

Also fixes:
- Hardcoded past dates (2025-06) replaced with dynamic dates (+90 days)
- Added anti-detection measures (webdriver flag, CDP override)
- Session/cookie management now handled automatically by the browser

feat: observability — Prometheus metrics, aletheia status CLI (#494)

## Summary

- **OBS-01:** `GET /metrics` Prometheus endpoint — HTTP, pipeline, LLM,
tool, and system metrics with LazyLock statics per crate, global
registry coordination, and path normalization middleware
- **OBS-03:** `aletheia status` CLI command — gateway health, nous
actors, disk storage with colored terminal output and graceful offline
fallback

## Metrics registered

| Category | Metrics |
|----------|---------|
| HTTP | `aletheia_http_requests_total{method,path,status}`,
`aletheia_http_request_duration_seconds{method,path}` |
| Pipeline | `aletheia_pipeline_turns_total{nous_id}`,
`aletheia_pipeline_stage_duration_seconds{nous_id,stage}`,
`aletheia_pipeline_errors_total{nous_id,stage,error_type}` |
| LLM | `aletheia_llm_tokens_total{provider,direction}`,
`aletheia_llm_cost_total{provider}`,
`aletheia_llm_requests_total{provider,status}` |
| Tools | `aletheia_tool_invocations_total{tool_name,status}`,
`aletheia_tool_duration_seconds{tool_name}` |
| System | `aletheia_active_sessions`, `aletheia_uptime_seconds` |

## Test plan

- [x] Zero clippy warnings (`cargo clippy --workspace --exclude
aletheia-mneme-engine --all-targets -- -D warnings`)
- [x] All 753 tests pass (`cargo test --workspace`)
- [ ] Manual: `curl http://localhost:18789/metrics` returns Prometheus
text format
- [ ] Manual: `aletheia status` shows formatted output
- [ ] Manual: `aletheia status` with pylon down shows disk-only info

---------
